Package edu.berkeley.cs.jqf.fuzz.jetcheck

Class JetCheckArgumentsGenerator

java.lang.Object
edu.berkeley.cs.jqf.fuzz.jetcheck.JetCheckArgumentsGenerator
All Implemented Interfaces:
ArgumentsGenerator
public final class JetCheckArgumentsGenerator extends Object implements ArgumentsGenerator
Generates one trial's arguments by running the per-parameter jetCheck generators against the engine's guided byte stream.

Each value is produced by JetCheckGeneration, so the bytes the engine supplies determine the arguments and replaying the same bytes rebuilds them. If a generator cannot produce a value for the current input, the whole trial is skipped.

A custom ArgumentsGeneratorFactory for its own parameter types can reuse this loop -- the per-parameter draw and the null/skip guard -- by building one through builder(Generator[]) with its own generators.

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_SIZE_HINT

      public static final int DEFAULT_SIZE_HINT
      The default size hint. It biases jetCheck's list and string lengths; the exact length is still drawn from the byte stream, this only sets the centre of that draw. jetCheck's own default cycles 1..100, so a fixed 16 keeps generated collections small.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • builder

      @SafeVarargs public static JetCheckArgumentsGenerator.Builder builder(org.jetbrains.jetCheck.Generator<?>... generators)
      Starts a builder for a generator over the given per-parameter jetCheck generators.
      Parameters:
      generators - one generator per test-method parameter, in declaration order
      Returns:
      a builder, defaulting to DEFAULT_SIZE_HINT

    • generate

      public Object[] generate(InputStream input)
      Description copied from interface: ArgumentsGenerator
      Generates one trial's arguments from the given input.

      The array length equals the test method's parameter count. Reading past the end of the input typically surfaces as a SkipTrialException, which the engine treats as a signal to move on to the next input rather than as a test failure.

      Specified by:
      generate in interface ArgumentsGenerator
      Parameters:
      input - the guided input bytes for this trial
      Returns:
      the arguments to pass to the test method
